idea: EMBEDDING_LR 0.4->0.8 (double embedding LR for faster embedding learning)
evidence: Undertrained regime: WD=0 success (6148421) showed less constraint helps. Faster embedding learning = better downstream features.
notes: Embeddings are input to everything — undertrained embeddings limit the whole model. Higher LR = faster feature learning. Risk: embedding instability (but muP should protect).

idea: Learnable logit multiplier before softcap: logits = softcap * tanh(m * logits / softcap) with m init 1.0
evidence: Softcap=15 is load-bearing (f5a9cd9 proved). c1d383e showed tighter softcap hurts. A learnable pre-softcap scalar lets model self-adjust effective logit range without changing softcap constant.
notes: 1 new scalar param. Zero-init would be identity-at-init if we use (1+m) form. Simple, low risk. Test if model benefits from effective softcap tuning during training.

evidence: Long-context research (arxiv 2310.05209 YaRN) shows higher RoPE base improves modeling at longer seqs. At seq_len=2048, base=10000 wastes precision on ultra-long relative dists. Partial RoPE means only 16 dims rotated so base tuning matters more.
notes: No param change. Risk: disrupts positional learning if base too extreme. 50000 is conservative (Llama3 uses 500000 at 8k+).

idea: Variance-Adaptive Muon: replace momentum_buffer.lerp_(g,1-m) with NSR-modulated momentum per arxiv 2601.14603
evidence: 2026 arxiv paper shows VAM beats tuned Muon+AdamW on GPT-2/LLaMA pretraining. We use Muon so transfer should be clean.
notes: Modify muon_step_fused kernel. Risk: torch.compile compat, numerical stability with MXFP8. Need careful implementation.

idea: Replace QK-norm with tanh soft-clipping: q = tanh(q), k = tanh(k) (norm-free attention bounds)
evidence: DyT (d475893) failed as full norm replacement, but applying tanh ONLY to q,k before scaled_dot_product may avoid RMSNorm removal issue while still bounding logits like softcap does at output.
notes: No param change. Different from DyT (only QK, not residual norms). Risk: tanh saturates pre-norm magnitudes, may lose info.
